name: Claims resolver & OIDC error types
outcome: The library owns the standard scope-to-claim mapping and the error types UserInfo needs, ready for the endpoint to consume.
scope: Add the OIDC scope-to-claim mapping plus a scope-gated filter, and the invalid_token and insufficient_scope error types. No endpoint wiring.
acceptance_criteria: The filter returns only claims authorized by the granted scopes, with sub always present
invalid_token and insufficient_scope exceptions are constructible and carry the correct error codes
steps: src/oidc — add the standard OIDC scope-to-claim mapping (profile, email, address, phone) per OIDC Core section 5.4
src/oidc — add a filter that reduces a claims object to those the granted scopes authorize, always retaining sub
OAuthException — add invalid_token and insufficient_scope error types with static helpers
test/unit — cover the filter across scope combinations and the new exception helpers
notes: The scope-to-claim mapping is spec-defined, so the library owns it rather than the consumer. The consumer's getUserClaims returns whatever attributes it has; this filter enforces what the granted scopes permit. Depends only on issue 1 — it touches neither the oidc config block nor anything from issue 2, so it can run in parallel with the 3-to-4 chain.
